items.summary:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.

What changed (text excerpt):
Added:
  + Education Home
  + Court History
  + Gignoux Courthouse Accessibility Information Handbook
  + Judges
  + Advisory RE: Personal Identifiers
  + Handout for Self-Represented Parties
  + Electronic Filing (ECF)
  + Information for Pro Se Parties
  + LOCAL RULES
  + To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software (Adobe 7 or higher).
  + Local Rules effective December 1, 2022
  + Interim Rule 4 – Maine Foreclosure Diversion Pilot
  + General Order 2021-5 : Interim Procedures for the Filing, Service, and Management of Highly Sensitive Documents
  + However, we remain open to suggestions and welcome comments you may have for improving our local rules. You may send them to us at:
  + localrules@med.uscourts.gov
  + .
Removed:
  - Education / Outreach
  - Accessibility & Accommodations
  - Judges and Court History
  - Advisory: Personal Identifiable Information
  - Information Packet for Self-Represented Parties
  - Electronic Filing
  - Legal Support Staff Materials
  - Case Information by Phone
  - Court Photos
  - To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software.
  - Local Rules effective April 1, 2025

items.title: Page updated: U.S. District Court, District of Maine — Local Rules

page_lines: Honorable Lance E. Walker, Chief Judge
Jennifer P. Lyons, Clerk of Court
Local Rules of Practice for the District of Maine
Where can I get legal advice?
Notice Re Third-Party Services and Access to Records in CM/ECF
To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software (Adobe 7 or higher).
Interim Rule 4 – Maine Foreclosure Diversion Pilot
General Order 2021-5 : Interim Procedures for the Filing, Service, and Management of Highly Sensitive Documents

It is our hope that these local rules will provide practitioners in our district with a clear statement of the Court's policies and procedures and that they will contribute to the improvement of administration of justice in our Court.
However, we remain open to suggestions and welcome comments you may have for improving our local rules. You may send them to us at:
In addition, you may contact any member of the Local Rules committee (contact information provided in the link below) to discuss your suggestions or questions about the Local Rules.
